questions: Q1: What is the complete report structure and assembly sequence for a 10-section narrative-arc research report?
Q2: What source documents and evidence categories must be synthesized, and in what priority order?
Q3: What are the key content decisions that shape how each section is written?
Q4: How should the verification plan validate report completeness and accuracy without code changes?

answers.answer: The report follows a 10-section narrative-arc structure optimized for pitch-deck conversion: (1) The Structural Gap (problem via three-way thesis from Positioning Refined), (2) Market Opportunity (TAM with three concentric rings), (3) What Helix Accomplishes (six high-level capability categories), (4) Traction & Momentum (production metrics with honest contextualization), (5) Financial Model (three-layer: base, stress-test, upside), (6) Competitive Landscape (concentric compression model), (7) Risk Register & Mitigations (Reality Check milestones as founder discipline), (8) Team & Investment (dual structure), (9) Branding & Positioning Language (context-organized lines), (10) Future Vision (ERP-as-database thesis). Assembly sequence: read all 7 PDFs first, gather supplementary runtime data if needed, write sections 1-10 in order, assemble into single report/report.md with executive summary and evidence appendix, self-verify.
evidence: tech-research/tech-research.md — narrative-arc structure chosen as Option B; 10-section content architecture table defined
product/product.md — success criteria list defining 10 required output categories
ticket.md — 'information that I'm going to feed in as a prompt to a pitch deck'

answers.answer: Seven PDF source documents organized by reliability tier: Tier 1 Manifesto (philosophical foundation), Tier 2 Dovie Offer (most recent financials), Tier 3 Positioning Refined + Transcript (thesis narrative), Tier 4 Reality Check (threat assessment with milestones), Tier 5 One-Pager (market sizing and team), Tier 6 Tagline (branding). Plus: six codebases for capability evidence at highest category level only, production runtime data (7 orgs, 22 users, 264 tickets, 124 deployed, 81% autonomous, 33 repos), and externally validated market intelligence from diagnosis web searches (NetSuite 43K+ customers, AI-in-ERP $5.82B->$58.7B, ERP consulting $50.1B). All seven PDFs must be re-read directly by the implementation step to avoid telephoning errors through intermediate summaries.
evidence: ticket.md — source priority ranking from founder
scout/scout-summary.md — six-tier evidence landscape with production metrics
diagnosis/diagnosis-statement.md — web search validated market data and competitive intelligence

answers.answer: Eight core content decisions from tech-research shape the report: (1) Six high-level capability categories anchored to Manifesto principles, no software implementations per user guidance; (2) Three-layer financial presentation (base, stress-test, upside) for investor transparency; (3) Three concentric TAM rings leading with conservative $600M floor; (4) Concentric compression competitive model ('governance is scarce') not a feature matrix; (5) Honest traction contextualization with proactive weak-metric framing; (6) Lead with structural gap not ERP-as-database vision (near-term problem grounds the pitch); (7) Reality Check milestones positioned as founder discipline with kill rules; (8) Branding language organized by destination (homepage, pitch, thesis, hook, boundary test) for direct lift.
evidence: tech-research/tech-research.md — Decisions 1-8 with rejected alternatives documented
User continuation context — 'you don't have to point out specific software implementations'
Reality_Check___Risks.pdf — milestone gates and kill rules

answers.answer: The verification plan has 8 Required Checks covering: (CHK-01) file existence with all 10 sections present, (CHK-02) narrative arc ordering, (CHK-03) financial figure accuracy against Dovie PDF (5 specific figures), (CHK-04) production metric accuracy against scout/diagnosis values (8 specific metrics), (CHK-05) no software implementation details in capability section, (CHK-06) branding language verbatim match against Tagline and Positioning Refined PDFs, (CHK-07) evidence quality tiering present with minimum 3 tiers, (CHK-08) all four financial stress-test points addressed. All checks use static file reading and cross-referencing since this is a research report with no runtime behavior to test. Pre-conditions: 7 PDFs accessible, prior artifacts available, runtime metrics available, output directory writable.
evidence: product/product.md — success criteria for the report deliverable
diagnosis/diagnosis-statement.md — stress-test points and production metrics to verify against

statement: The implementation plan directs creation of a single research report at report/report.md in the helix-cli run root. The report has 10 modular sections following a narrative-arc structure (Structural Gap -> Market -> Capabilities -> Traction -> Financials -> Competition -> Risks -> Team -> Branding -> Vision) optimized for dual use as pitch-deck prompt material and website content source. Seven PDF source documents (Manifesto, Dovie Offer, Positioning Refined, Positioning Transcript, Reality Check, One-Pager, Tagline) are synthesized alongside production runtime data and externally validated market intelligence. Product capabilities are described at six high-level categories without software implementation details, anchored to Manifesto principles. The financial model uses a three-layer presentation (base case, stress-test, upside framing). The competitive landscape uses a concentric compression model centered on 'governance is scarce.' Evidence quality is tiered (hard facts, sourced, projections, unverifiable). The verification plan has 8 Required Checks covering completeness, accuracy, implementation-detail compliance, and evidence quality. No code changes are needed in any repository.
